How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-09 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing in 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day, leveraging validated signal-stack combinations for excess return.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CAT at score 190 hits three screens (CLENOW_MOMENTUM, EUPHORIA, PULLBACK), but the EUPHORIA tag paired with a pullback read creates mechanical tension between extended price and a retracement setup. MS at 176 is the cleaner name: momentum plus variance premium in a financial fits a defined-risk entry with no internal signal contradiction. No ticker in the batch matched a validated combo, so all 10 names sit in generic 3-screen overlap territory rather than the higher-conviction VALID buckets such as SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3 or the 3-plus VALID cluster. With only n_overlaps=20 and scores ranging from 190 down to 95 across the top 10, the batch is broad and thin rather than concentrated at the top. NEUTRAL macro at 48.62 adds no directional lift; MS is first look at moderate conviction, sized tight.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: TRV (score 5, target no v2 estimate).

0 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects informed options flow via unusual sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta shift, EMA8/21 trend structure, and low-OI anomalies to isolate smart money positioning.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1QQQ ↑50 Watch$722.38 -0.12%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The monthly cohorts report 100% win rates across April, May, and June 2026, but with only 5 of 295 total signals resolved and 0% expired, virtually no positions have closed, so the live tape cannot confirm or contradict the backtest's 68.1% win rate and 2.39 Sharpe - the WATCH verdict exists precisely because the resolved sample is 75% short of the 20-trade minimum.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 295 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin: one signal, QQQ at score 50 in Watch tier with a BULLISH SWEEP pattern, sitting at the floor of the scoring range with nothing clearing 65 or 80. A single name at the minimum threshold means the screener found marginal confirmation, not conviction. Macro is NEUTRAL at 48.62, which neither blocks nor supports a QQQ long. With a score distribution that is a single data point at the bottom of the range, this batch is weak - if pressed, QQQ first at the lowest possible conviction, sized small.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the backtest rather than contradicting it: 72% win rate, +3.4% average against SPY at -1.0%, and monthly cohorts trending from 50.8% in April up to 86.1% in June show the edge is strengthening, not decaying, with the June cohort's 86.1% the number that matters most.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XEL and PCG share the day's top score at 77, both Signal-tier utilities collecting the full Sinclair +31% sector bias adjustment, though pattern data is null for both so the case rests on the IV/HV spread and value factors alone. DUK follows at 76 and the entire top-8 is either utilities or CPB in consumer staples at 75, making this a sector-concentrated print, not a broad market opportunity. All 36 signals compress tightly between 65 and 77 with nothing clearing 80, so the distribution is clustered and top-heavy only in relative terms, not in absolute score strength. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 neither accelerates nor undermines short-vol positioning, which is about as uninspiring a backdrop as you can get. Lean XEL first over PCG at medium conviction only, the tied score gives no separation and the lack of pattern confirmation keeps sizing conservative.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

BMRN at 100 and ANAB at 99 lead the batch, both Very strong tier, making them the cleanest VRP-aligned setups on today's screen. The batch skews heavily healthcare, with JNJ at 97 and ABBV at 95 adding large-cap depth to the biotech exposure, and pattern data is null across all names so the entry case rests on VRP score alone. Distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 75, meaning this is a concentrated quality batch rather than a flat wash. Macro at NEUTRAL (48.62) poses no regime headwind for a mid-VRP long strategy. BMRN is the first look at high conviction - perfect score, biotech optionality, no obvious catalyst overhang baked into pricing by construction.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is ceiling-saturated to the point of being nearly undifferentiated - 44 of 50 signals score 80 or above, median sits at 100, and every listed pick scores a perfect 100 with no pattern field populated, which removes any within-batch ranking based on screener data alone. CVLT and GLOB are the most liquid names in the list, making their put-flow ratios more interpretable as genuine institutional hedging versus micro-cap biotechs like CAPR or ESPR, where a single market maker can inflate the ratio artificially. The macro regime is neutral at 48.62, neither amplifying nor undermining the contrarian thesis. Lean toward CVLT first given the larger float and cleaner institutional signal read, at moderate conviction only.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms the backtest: 63.9% WR and +3.93% avg beating SPY by 2.5 points tracks tightly against the backtested 65% WR, and monthly cohorts show a clear recovery arc from the February wash (24.6% WR, avg -3.3%) through June at 60.2%, with the 2.5-point SPY spread being the key validation number.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 63% · avg +4.2% · beats SPY +2.0% by +2.2 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 491 resolved at +20d · WR 63% · avg +4.2% · vs SPY +2.0%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 496 closed · WR TB 47% · 18% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

TFII and VCTR both score 95 (Very strong, LAUNCH) and are the sharpest setups in today's batch, with CRSR at 90 adding a third name carrying the same pattern and tier. The score distribution is unusually compressed at the top: all 50 signals score 80 or above with a median of 80, which signals a high-quality batch but collapses any differentiation below the Very strong names. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 is a partial drag - pullback reversals need a risk-on bid to fire cleanly, and this tape is not providing one. Take TFII first at high conviction, treat VCTR as a same-day second entry, and keep position size one step below max given the regime score.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LAC and PPTA both score 94 (Very strong) and are the clearest entries today, with TMC at 91 rounding out that top tier. No pattern data exists for any name, so the signal rests on score and tier placement alone. The distribution is top-heavy and dense: 19 of 25 signals clear 80, median at 84, which puts today's batch in structurally solid shape. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (48.62), posing no directional tailwind for a contrarian reversion play but no active headwind either. Lead with LAC as the first basket leg at low conviction per name, 1-2% sizing as the construction mandates.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity and weekend effect, flagging sell-vol setups at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$752.50 +0.11%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape shows a 67% win rate and +1.4% avg versus SPY's +1.4% baseline, meaning realized alpha is essentially zero (+0.0 pts), and the monthly cohort trend is deteriorating - April ran 100% WR at +5.4% avg, May slipped to 61% at +0.95%, and June's four signals averaged -0.29%, which contradicts the headline KEEP verdict and is the number that matters most right now.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name: SPY at a score of 61, Watch tier, no pattern attached. The score clears neither the 65 nor the 80 thresholds, n_ge_65 and n_ge_80 are both zero, so the distribution is as thin as this screener ever produces. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 does not set the conditions for a vol-climax environment where this signal earns its keep - the screener is designed to catch fear exhaustion, and a neutral tape offers no fear to exhaust. If forced to act, SPY LONG is the only candidate, but conviction is low and the correct move is to let this one sit.

🧠 Detects fresh options positioning - Vol/OI above 5 isolates new money - in calm, non-extended names where unusually large premium flow precedes directional moves.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

GLD scores 88, the only name at or above 80 and the sole entry at or above 65, but its BEARISH SWEEP pattern against a LONG direction label is a conflicted read shared by three other names in today's batch including UAL and QQQ. COIN at 61 carries a clean BULLISH SWEEP and is the most directionally coherent setup of the group. The score distribution is top-heavy in structure but thin in conviction, with seven of ten names clustering between 54 and 61 and a median of 56, which is a weak batch by this screener's standards. A NEUTRAL macro at 48.62 provides no directional bias and fits a session dominated by ambiguous sweep patterns and hedged positioning. First preference goes to COIN at low conviction given its cleaner flow-direction alignment, with GLD held at watch until the bearish sweep conflicts with the long thesis resolves.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLE scores 95 and hits Very strong tier, the only name in today's batch where the screener is making a real call. Score stats show two names at or above 80, but only XLE appears in the listed picks, leaving the second high scorer unidentified in the data. XLV at 65 earns the Signal tag and is the next cleanest read; six other names cluster between 50 and 60 in Watch, producing a top-heavy, single-spike distribution rather than a broad rotation signal. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 provides no tailwind, framing XLE as a sector-specific call rather than a market-wide momentum read. Take XLE first at moderate conviction given the absent pattern data on all names; XLV is a distant second.

🧠 Flags stocks off 40%-plus from 52-week highs with capitulation volume and a reversal candle, targeting mean-reversion within 15 days.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at 59.7% WR and +6.6% average beating SPY by 5.5 points confirms the backtest directionally, but monthly cohorts swinging from January's -12.9% average to June's +4.7% show the regime sensitivity the MARGINAL verdict flagged, with the 5.5-point SPY spread as the key number to track.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RGC is the only name at a perfect 100 score, Very strong tier, and warrants first look. IVA and WW both land at 80 in the Strong tier, but live tracking shows Strong tier averaging -0.47% versus Signal tier's +10.4%, so the tier label is misleading - the signal cluster anchored by LENZ at 78 carries the better live track record. The batch is broad but thin at the top, with 36 of 50 names at 65 or above and only 3 clearing 80. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 gives no directional tailwind for reversal plays. Preference is RGC first at high conviction, then LENZ as the best Signal tier entry given that tier's demonstrably superior live performance.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Captures trend quality via annualized regression slope times R-squared on 90 days, gated by SPY above MA200 and price above MA100.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin at two names: NKE at a perfect score of 100 (Very strong, LONG) and GIS at 77 (Signal, LONG). The score distribution is sparse - 2 signals total, 1 above 80 - not a day to build around. The critical problem is a live tier inversion: the Very strong tier carries a 30% win rate and avg -11.87% over 20 days, while the Signal tier runs 66.7% and avg +2.6%, meaning NKE's top score is actually the least favorable designation in real results. A neutral macro regime (48.62) offers no additional tailwind for a short-duration momentum continuation. Given the tier inversion, GIS is the first preference despite the lower score, but conviction is low and position sizing should reflect that.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian LONG on SPY when two or more major intermarket correlation pairs break by 2 sigma or more over 7 days.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Catches stocks with positive EPS surprise and gap-up that holds, scored on surprise size, gap, post-gap action, and analyst revisions.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$11.31 -1.05%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms the screener works better than its marginal backtest suggests - 58% win rate and +4.1% avg vs SPY's +0.1% over the same window is a 4.0-point edge that is hard to dismiss - but June 2026's cohort (WR 39%, avg +0.12%) is a sharp deterioration that warns the recent regime is eating into that edge.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name: BB at 95 (Very strong, PRIMED), which is both the max and the median - the distribution is not clustered, it is just one ticker. A one-name batch in a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 48.62) is not a blank check; concentration risk is the entire story here. BB's score is legitimate by the screener's own criteria, but the absence of a supporting cohort makes this a half-size starter at best. Take BB first if you take anything, and treat the thin batch as the real signal about today's earnings tape.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape's 76% win rate and +5.1% 20-day average, beating SPY by 3.9 points, validates the KEEP call and runs ahead of the informal backtest's modest signal, but May's cohort at 49% win rate and -1.1% average is the key number - the edge inverts fast in a weak tape.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals today scored 100 with a median of 100, meaning the scoring adds zero discrimination - this is a ceiling flush, not a ranked list. APLD and AESI are the higher-beta names within the Very Strong tier, where 200-day reclaims tend to produce larger follow-through; AGCO adds cyclical breadth as an agriculture-equipment re-entry. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 48.62, a moderate suppressor for broad momentum plays that need a constructive tape. Pattern data is null across all picks, removing a useful secondary filter. Start with APLD at low conviction and wait for one session of confirmed follow-through before adding size.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no rigorous backtest to anchor against (all stats are null, OBSERVATION only), the 90-day live tape is the sole evidence, and at 58% WR and +4.3% avg it confirms a real edge over SPY's +1.1%, with the monthly cohort recovery from April's 44% WR and -0.73% avg back to June's 58% WR and +4.6% avg validating continuation - but the Very strong tier's -6.4% average across 17 trades is the key number that directly undermines today's top-scoring names.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is top-heavy with five names - ACHC, ANAB, BEAM, BV, DFH - all scoring 95 and one more (CCCC) at 90, every one of them in the Very strong tier. That tier is the worst live performer across 17 resolved trades: 23.5% WR, avg -6.4%, suggesting maximally flagged setups attract too much attention or the squeeze is already spent by the time the signal fires. ABEO and ABX (score 85, Strong tier) sit in the bucket that actually delivers - 68.4% WR, avg +5.8% over 20 days. All 50 signals score at or above 65 with a median of 75 and 16 above 80, so the batch is densely packed near the top rather than thin, but that packing is not a quality signal here given the tier inversion. Macro is neutral at 48.62, which does not actively support squeeze dynamics, so take ABEO or ABX first at moderate conviction and avoid the score-95 cluster entirely until the Very strong tier performance turns around in live data.

🧠 Ranks RUT-1000 stocks by relative volume across three lookbacks times four-horizon price momentum, capturing institutional accumulation preceding trend continuation.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ALNY, BBIO, and MRNA lead at score 100, with AMAT, ANET, COMP, GTLS, and KYMR at 99, but all eight carry the same Very Strong tier and no pattern tag, so the screener provides no intra-batch ranking signal. The distribution is fully saturated - all 50 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 95, which means scoring has collapsed into a ceiling cluster rather than producing a genuine spread. Macro is NEUTRAL at 48.62, neither a tailwind nor a headwind. With the tracking verdict stamped KILL and no pattern differentiation to lean on, conviction across the batch is low; if one name must be prioritized, MRNA at score 100 has the most accessible near-term catalyst surface, but that judgment comes from outside the screener, not from it.

🧠 Flags large-premium (>$200K), short-dated OTM block trades on quiet names, filtering for genuine accumulation via next-day OI delta confirmation.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.

HEEM (95), CWAN (93), and TDSC (91) lead today's batch, all rated Very strong but with no pattern detail supplied for any of them. Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Catches rejection-tail bars at least 1.8x ATR at Market Profile value-area extremes, where price excess historically snaps back.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $TROO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1TROO ↑100●●● Very strong$1.93 +3.21%LOW2.2726.4%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Five resolved trades show 80% win rate and a +4.44% average 20-day return against SPY at -0.27%, and the June 2026 cohort at 100% win rate and +5.33% average is directionally in line with the backtest's +3.18% filtered result - but the WATCH verdict stands because 5 resolved trades is far below the 20 needed to treat these figures as reliable signal rather than favorable small-sample noise.

TROO is the only actionable name - take it first if you trade this screen, but size it conservatively given the null pattern data and the one-line batch.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Identifies post-earnings gap-ups that pull back on a weekly red candle and re-trigger on a weekly close above the prior week's high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.
Top picks: $BABA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago
1BABA ↑100●●● Very strong$113.73 +2.33%9.6%2.971
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

BABA is the only name in today's batch, scoring 100 in the Very strong tier with no pattern tag on file. One signal is not a distribution - it is a thin tape, and a median of 100 on n equals 1 tells you nothing about relative quality across candidates. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 neither adds conviction nor signals outright avoidance. Against a screener carrying a KILL verdict and a June cohort that averaged minus 14.5% on a 29% win rate, BABA at score 100 is the only name to track here, sized small if acted on at all, with first entry deferred until the tape shows follow-through.

🧠 Detects smart-flow prints and skips any setup where KVO or Force Index crosses within 3 days, treating volume signals as a negative filter.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape flatly contradicts the backtest: 90-day win rate of 40.2% and average return of -4.4% versus SPY's -0.8% (-3.5 pts of underperformance) against a backtest win rate of 52.7% and mean PnL of +3.4%, and the June 2026 cohort (25% WR, -14.5% avg) confirms the deterioration is recent and accelerating.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 519 closed · WR TB 39% · 9% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

AGL is the lone standout at score 100 and Very strong tier, the only name clearing the 80-point threshold in a 41-signal batch where the median sits at 17. Everything else is Watch tier: ABVX at 50, HYMC at 36, and five names clustered between 26 and 33 with no pattern data on any of them. The distribution is acutely top-heavy, one real signal surrounded by noise. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 48.62, which is marginal for a screener requiring SPY above SMA50, and the live record on Very strong names is ugly at 37% win rate and -4.3% average. Take AGL as the only candidate worth tracking, at low conviction only, and leave the Watch tier entirely.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Flags deeply oversold US equities using a weekly RSI below 30 gate, confirmed by daily RSI crossover, candlestick reversal, and demethod zone scoring.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $DOW $DKL $BCE

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape supports holding this screener despite the doctrine-rejected backtest, with 54.5% WR and avg +4.5% across 495 resolved trades beating SPY by 4.7 points, though the monthly cohort trend is a concern, deteriorating steadily from a March WR of 78.7% down to June at 54.2%, suggesting signal quality is compressing as the oversold universe expands.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LXU leads at a score of 77 as the only TRIGGER-stage Signal in the batch, meaning the reversal crossover conditions are actively firing, not merely setting up. DKL at 66 is the one other Signal-tier name but sits in SETUP, so the entry clock has not started. The score distribution is compressed, median 54, max 77, zero names clearing 80 and only two clearing 65, which is a thin, low-conviction spread rather than a top-heavy cluster. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 provides no directional tailwind for these contrarian longs, and with the weekly oversold gate coded to the June 9 close, some of that weekly exhaustion may already be partially digested. LXU is the first and only real focus here, small size, given it is the lone name with an active trigger in a batch that is otherwise early and soft.

🧠 Flags unusual volume, premium %, and OI delta on crypto-linked ETFs, scoring by flow intensity and spot-crypto correlation to surface positioning shifts ahead of price.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $MARA $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1MARA ↑50 Watch$13.59 +2.80%45.26-2.6%neutral
2MSTR ↑50 Watch$99.00 +5.44%40.63-17.9%neutral
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape fully confirms the kill verdict rather than contradicting the already-rejected backtest: 44.7% win rate and a negative 4.5% average return versus SPY's negative 0.1%, with the key number being June 2026's cohort going 0-for-24 at a negative 19.6% average, the sharpest drawdown month in this window and a direct reversal of May's 60.8% win rate.

IDNA leads the batch at a score of 70, the only name with any real separation, followed by SMIN at 67 and CLOU at 66. Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 35% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names hit max score (100, Very strong): BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM. SRTY is a leveraged inverse small-cap ETF, so a LONG signal there reads as a short-small-cap bet, not a standard equity long, and its presence in an all-long batch is an immediate flag. The distribution skews high, with 41 of 50 signals above 65 and 12 above 80, but no pattern data is attached to any pick, which strips out entry precision. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (48.62), adding no directional tailwind to what is already a crowded top bucket. Counterintuitively, the Very strong tier carries the weakest recent avg return (+3.43%) versus Watch (+9.54%), so max score is not the cleanest filter today - prioritise CELH or BL first at moderate conviction, given their sector legibility versus the rest of the batch.

🧠 Aggregates quarterly 13F position changes from 20 top hedge funds, scoring new buys and size increases, with a 60-to-90-day structural lag.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names that cleared 80, both in the Very strong tier, implying multiple whale funds opened or expanded positions in the same quarter. AVGO at 79 falls just below that cut, with HUT, TSM, MU, and V clustered tightly between 74 and 77. The distribution is top-heavy: median sits at 55, only 2 of 50 signals clear 80, and 13 clear 65, so conviction thins out fast beneath the top two. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 provides no directional tailwind, and the inherent 60-to-90-day staleness of 13F data means every position here could already be stale or partially unwound. ASML is the first call at high conviction, as the score gap over the rest of the batch is meaningful and large-fund semis exposure has been sticky.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and COE both hit 100 while PBLS and CADEX land at 99, but the score distribution exposes a ceiling-compression problem: all 46 signals clear 80 and the median is 94, which means the ranking carries almost no discrimination. Pattern fields are null across the board, so you have dollar size and rank but no technical or timing context to separate the names. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 adds no directional lift, meaning insider conviction is doing all the work with no macro tailwind behind it. JEF at 98 is the only name in the batch with real liquidity and institutional tradability, which makes it the first look for any sizing of consequence. Take JEF at moderate conviction, treat SVRE as a small satellite if the position size fits the float.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Screens for lowest EV/EBIT in the US equity universe, surfacing the cheapest businesses by operating earnings yield as a mean-reversion vehicle.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With 0 of 53 tracked picks resolved and monthly cohorts empty, the tape cannot confirm or contradict the backtest (which does not exist), leaving only 29 tiebreaker observations at a 58.6% rate - too thin to read as anything other than noise.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (0 resolved, need ≥20)

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 30 closed · WR TB 57% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ scores a perfect 100 and WRD prints 97, both in the Very strong tier, making them the two names that sit cleanest against the multiple screen. GPC at 90 adds a large-cap anchor with durable operating earnings, rounding out the top cluster. The distribution is top-heavy: 7 of 16 names clear 80 and the median sits at 73, so this is not a scattered or thin batch. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 is neither a drag nor a boost for deep value, and the absence of any pattern data across all eight names limits the setup further. BZ is the first preference on pure score, but conviction stays low with no validated backtest and no pattern confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape on 441 resolved signals - 61% win rate, +5.2% average 20-day return, beating SPY by 5.6 percentage points - and the June and May cohorts (60% and 62% win rates, +9.5% and +4.7% averages) confirm the signal carries real directional edge, though April's 44% win rate at a 539% best-case distorted average is a reminder that cohort averages can be misleading and the signal weakens in choppy tape.

Pattern data is null across the board, so there is no technical context layered on top of the Form 4 signal to help triage. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 48.62, which neither amplifies nor suppresses the screener's baseline tendency, leaving the batch to stand on weak footing alone. If forced to act, KMX is the first look on liquidity grounds alone, but this is a low-conviction day and the right call is to wait for a batch with at least one name clearing 65.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Scores individual laggards inside LATE_CHASE and RIDING sectors by multiplying sector relative strength against stock underperformance, targeting catch-up momentum.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 Antonacci dual momentum on macro ETFs: absolute 12-month return must be positive AND the ETF must rank top among peers before a long signal fires.
Construction
Antonacci dual momentum on a macro ETF set: absolute (12m return > 0) AND relative (top rank vs peers). Long the leaders.

Intraday ~30 min
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects retail-driven euphoria with no earnings catalyst, scoring social attention, call/put skew, and price stretch to flag SHORT candidates.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $ACN $CAT $CF
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1ACN ↓100●●● Very strong$140.78 +1.24%FOMO1.23-5.0%
2CAT ↓100●●● Very strong$934.93 -0.37%FOMO1.152.2%
3CF ↓100●●● Very strong$114.73 +0.62%FOMO1.64-0.4%
4ASND ↓83●● Strong$276.91 +0.54%EUPHORIA0.831.4%
5ASTS ↓52 Watch$73.29 -0.80%GREED0.52-0.1%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Tracking contradicts any meaningful short edge and makes the absence of a formal backtest irrelevant - the 90-day resolved sample shows only +0.2 pts over SPY at a 56% win rate, the options book collapses to 31.7% wins with 15.5% expiring worthless, and the monthly cohorts reinforce deterioration, with April at 16.7% win rate and -15.9% average and June already sliding to 48.5% and -2.7%, against a May outlier of 62.5% that looks like noise in context.

🧠 Ranks tickers by 20-day relative strength versus SPY, sector breadth, and cluster position to surface sector rotation leaders.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$25.23 +0.64%Real Estate19.0%31.5%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Pre-earnings straddles where implied vol is below Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, capturing realized volatility that options markets have underpriced.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $JPM $WFC $DHR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1JPM ↑65● Signal$337.26 +0.53%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2WFC ↑65● Signal$87.75 +0.97%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3DHR ↑55 Watch$195.44 -0.28%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
4GE ↑55 Watch$355.10 -1.10%PRE_IV_RAMP9—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

JPM and WFC lead at 65, both reaching the Signal tier and standing as the only two names above the median in a four-signal batch. The distribution is compressed and capped low, with nothing above 65 and the bottom two names, DHR and GE, sitting at 55 Watch with null pattern data across the board. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 48.62 neither helps nor hurts a pure vol mispricing play because the edge is event-driven, not trend-dependent. JPM gets first priority over WFC at low-to-moderate conviction, given deeper options liquidity in the large-cap bank names heading into earnings. This is a thin batch and sizing should reflect it.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bull traps where price breaks above the value area high then closes back inside, flagging trapped longs as short entries.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ANNX $AVB $ATEC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ANNX ↓80●● Strong$5.78 -2.69%BULL_TRAP6.625.34
2AVB ↓75● Signal$190.69 +0.65%BULL_TRAP196.98185.97
3ATEC ↓60 Watch$9.40 -1.16%BULL_TRAP9.367.46
4AZTA ↓55 Watch$25.45 +0.14%BULL_TRAP25.6218.40
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest exists yet (N=0, needs 30 resolved trades), so the 90-day live tape is the only evidence, and it returns a KILL: 56% win rate, -0.46% average P&L versus SPY's -0.61% for a 0.1 percentage point edge that is noise, with the May cohort sliding to 33% win rate pointing the wrong direction.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects false breaks of value area boundaries - a close below VAL reclaimed next day signals trapped shorts and a forced squeeze.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ASHR $AVGX $AVL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Put/Call Skew Spike

IV_SKEWEODData: 2026-07-10
Live: IV_SKEW

FULL SIZE OK
WR 55%
Refresh
EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put IV spikes above the 90-day baseline, reading extreme hedging demand as a contrarian long signal when price action stays calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR $META
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑61 Watch$99.00 +5.44%66%990.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
2META ↓51 Watch$670.92 +6.25%55%650.21SELL PUT (put skew rich)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape posts 54% WR and +7.6% average (beating SPY by 6.4 pts, verdict KEEP), but the key number is the tier inversion - Watch signals average +13.9% while Very strong signals average -5.9%, and June's cohort has turned negative at -2.2%, suggesting the aggregate headline overstates current edge.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

Today's batch is thin - 3 signals, median 68, only one name clearing 80, and patterns are null across the board with no technical confirmation layer. NEUTRAL macro at 48.62 adds neither tailwind nor headwind to the contrarian read. Start with META as a low-size LONG over MSTR on tier pedigree, skip COST entirely until the Very strong tier shows evidence of stabilising in live data.

🧠 Composite screener detecting vol mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure slope, and Kerberos-style flow scoring.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $SVXY $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1SVXY ↓85●● Strong$58.07 +0.28%29%926%RULE_1
2VXX ↓85●● Strong$21.48 -0.47%49%354%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV sits above the 90th percentile of skew, capturing unusual hedging demand that can signal sentiment washout and set up contrarian longs.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$398.65 -0.61%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Nine resolved trades produce a 55.6% overall win rate but a -5.7% average 20-day return against SPY at +0.77%, driven almost entirely by the Strong tier's 0% win rate and -55.9% average on two trades, a result that directly contradicts any positive read on tier quality and keeps the verdict firmly at WATCH pending the minimum 20 resolved trades needed for even a tentative conclusion.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 minus HV20 spikes above 1.5 standard deviations on a 90-day lookback, targeting elevated fear premium as a premium-selling candidate.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1SPY ↓61 Watch$752.50 +0.11%23%15%1.54FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The monthly cohort trend contradicts the 90-day tracking aggregate of WR 43% and avg +1.4% versus SPY +1.1%: April ran avg +10.8%, May fell to avg -0.2%, June collapsed to avg -3.3%, and that June number is the one that matters, sitting 4.4 points below the SPY baseline and signaling the edge embedded in the 90-day read is fading, with no formal backtest yet available to anchor how much deterioration is noise versus regime shift.

🧠 Flags US equities by Altman Z-Score, screening for financial distress or turnaround setups using balance sheet and earnings ratios.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

🧠 STFR screens equities against proprietary criteria; the construction script is not exposed in this payload, so the mechanism is opaque.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags institutional supply and demand imbalances by reading price spread, close location, and volume together to front-run professional money flow.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
